title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Qatar's former ruler Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani has died at 74
stories.summary:
Qatar's former ruler Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani has died at 74. Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, who as ruler of Qatar transformed the tiny Persian Gulf nation into a global player in diplomacy, media and investment, and then shattered tradition by voluntarily turning over power to his son, has died, state media reported.

He was 74. Al Jazeera: How will Qatar’s Father Emir be remembered?

Al Jazeera reports that former Emir Sheikh Hamad Bin Khalifa Al Thani has died.

links.headline: How will Qatar’s Father Emir be remembered?
links.excerpt: Former Emir Sheikh Hamad Bin Khalifa Al Thani has died
links.imageAlt: How will Qatar’s Father Emir be remembered?

stories.title: Patel says FBI helping local authorities after Lindsey Graham’s sudden death
stories.summary:
Patel says FBI helping local authorities after Lindsey Graham’s sudden death. FBI Director Kash Patel said on Sunday his agency is assisting local authorities in Washington, D.C., after four-term Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-SC), a close ally of President Donald Trump, died Saturday night following a “brief and sudden illness.” “The FBI is assisting local authorities and has made every necessary resource available,” Patel said in a […].

BBC: US senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'. BBC reports that a spokesman for the 71-year-old South Carolina senator says initial findings suggest Graham's cause of death was related to his heart.

stories.title: US launches additional strikes on Iran after Tehran broadened attacks in the Gulf
stories.summary:
US launches additional strikes on Iran after Tehran broadened attacks in the Gulf. The United States Central Command said Sunday afternoon it launched retaliatory strikes against Iran after Tehran attacked multiple Gulf states earlier Sunday and fired at vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

“At 5 p.m.

E.T today, U.S. Central Command forces began launching more strikes against Iran to continue degrading their ability to attack civilian mariners […]. BBC: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open as it exchanges strikes with Iran.

BBC reports that iranian forces earlier said the Strait was closed and launched wide-ranging attacks at US allies and bases in the region.

stories.title: US launches further strikes on Iran as the two nations disagree over status of Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary:
US launches further strikes on Iran as the two nations disagree over status of Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. military launched a new round of strikes against Iran on Sunday evening, as Washington looks to hamper the Islamist regime's ability to control the Strait of Hormuz.

Al Jazeera: US launches more strikes on Iran as Strait of Hormuz standoff deepens.

Al Jazeera reports that the US has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran targeting Tehran's ability to threaten shipping in the strait.

stories.title: McConnell says a fall led to hospitalization and he won’t return to Senate floor ‘quite yet’
stories.summary:
McConnell says a fall led to hospitalization and he won’t return to Senate floor ‘quite yet’. Sen. Mitch McConnell (R-KY) broke weeks of silence about his health on Sunday, saying he was hospitalized last month after he “took a fall” and he doesn’t expect “to return to the Senate floor to vote quite yet.” In a statement addressed to his constituents, McConnell revealed it was a fall that landed him in […].

BBC: US Senator Mitch McConnell says absence due to fall and pneumonia. BBC reports that the veteran Republican says he will not return to work "quite yet", in his first statement following weeks of speculation about his health.

stories.title: At least 27 killed, eight critically injured in Bangkok bar fire
stories.summary:
At least 27 killed, eight critically injured in Bangkok bar fire. Firefighters arrived at the scene in the Chatuchak district to find patrons running through flames to escape.

Al Jazeera: At least 27 dead as fire engulfs popular Bangkok pub near Chatuchak market.

Al Jazeera reports that at least 27 people were killed and 63 injured, many critically, after a fire ripped through a popular pub in Bangkok.

stories.title: At least 27 dead in Bangkok after fire breaks out at bar: Officials
stories.summary:
At least 27 dead in Bangkok after fire breaks out at bar: Officials. At least 27 people are dead and another 22 are in critical condition after a fire at a bar in Bangkok, Thailand, erupted early Monday morning local time.

A large blaze engulfed the Na Ladprao pub in the northern part of the nation’s capital, sending patrons fleeing into the bathrooms at the back of the […].

Fox News: Massive fire rips through pub, killing at least 27 as patrons flee smoke-filled venue. Fox News reports that officials said the fire broke out around midnight at a northern Bangkok pub, sending thick smoke through the venue as patrons tried to escape.

stories.title: Classical pianist Jayson Gillham loses discrimination case against MSO over Gaza comments – video
stories.summary:
Classical pianist Jayson Gillham loses discrimination case against MSO over Gaza comments – video. A classical pianist who sued the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra has lost his case after he alleged he was unlawfully discriminated against because of his views on Israeli forces killing Palestinian journalists.

Gillham had claimed the MSO’s cancellation of a Melbourne concert he was contracted to perform on 15 August 2024 was an attempt to silence him over his stance on Israel’s attacks in Gaza. Justice Graeme Hill handed judgment down on Friday, finding that the MSO would have taken the same a.

stories.title: A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed
stories.summary: A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed. HyperTexting's new app aims to make the open web feel more like social media by turning websites, blogs, newsletters, and podcasts into a scrollable feed, while also making it easier to post to your own website.

stories.title: ‘These are some of the most complex structures ever created’: how tech reporting moved into the physical world
stories.summary:
‘These are some of the most complex structures ever created’: how tech reporting moved into the physical world. The Guardian’s global tech reporting team are investigating the impact of the vast datacentres being built to power the AI revolution.

We spoke to them about how their beat has become increasingly offline Journalists often use the term “shoe-leather reporting” to refer to the on-the-ground legwork that goes into covering certain stories.

As the tech industry’s focus has shifted from screen-based realities to the physical world of colossal AI datacentres and social media harms, comfortab.

stories.title: What is Paralives? The creative life simulator game that could rival The Sims
stories.summary:
What is Paralives? The creative life simulator game that could rival The Sims.

With players leaving EA’s series once life there felt like a grind beset by ethical concerns, this quirky new sim promises a better life elsewhere For 26 years, the life-sims genre has been dominated by one series: The Sims.

Originally designed by Will Wright, creator of Sim City, EA’s virtual dollhouse series has grown into a $5bn [£3.8bn] empire with the constant release of new games, expansion packs, and collaborations cementing its place among the bestselling video game franchises o.

stories.title: Magnitude 4.8 earthquake hits off Masbate coastal town
stories.summary:
Magnitude 4.8 earthquake hits off Masbate coastal town. MANILA, Philippines — A magnitude 4.8 earthquake struck off a coastal town in Masbate on Monday morning, state seismologists said.

The tectonic earthquake occurred 40 kilometers southwest of Claveria town at 8:53 a.m., with a shallow depth of seven kilometers, according to the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs). Phivolcs said instrumental intensities were.

stories.title: Can Amenah Pangandaman continue reforms in PH’s sole Islamic bank?
stories.summary:
Can Amenah Pangandaman continue reforms in PH’s sole Islamic bank?.

MANILA, Philippines — Former Budget Secretary Amenah Pangandaman has been named the chair and chief executive officer of the P2.6-billion state-owned Al-Amanah Islamic Investment Bank of the Philippines (AAIIBP), marking her return to the government months after she resigned from the Cabinet last year.

The country’s only Islamic bank announced Pangandaman’s appointment on July 10.

stories.title: NBI recommends raps vs Tab Baldwin, 11 others over Ateneo cagers’ deaths
stories.summary:
NBI recommends raps vs Tab Baldwin, 11 others over Ateneo cagers’ deaths.

MANILA, Philippines — MANILA, Philippines — The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has recommended that the Department of Justice (DOJ) file charges against resigned Ateneo de Manila University men’s basketball team head coach Tab Baldwin and 11 others.

The recommendation came after the NBI Homicide Division completed its investigation into the drowning that killed Ateneo.

stories.title: Chasing new skills, going back to basics and pushing for collective action: how software engineers are adapting to AI
stories.summary:
Chasing new skills, going back to basics and pushing for collective action: how software engineers are adapting to AI. Software engineering was one of the best-paying professions in the US in 2022, but the advent of AI has disrupted it, leading to several layoffs and underemployment Every weekday, Matt, a software engineer, looks forward to his four-hour train commute to Pawling, New York.

It’s time he uses to work on his own project: a browser-based video game for which he writes every line of code himself. “I am actively trying to keep my axe sharp,” said Matt, who did not want to use his actual.
